meta.title: The Clever Monkey — Vocabulary Set 3
meta.subject: English Vocabulary
meta.description: Week 4 vocabulary focus: fairly, swung, thanked, believe, afraid, exactly, carefully, sadly.

questions.question: What does 'fairly' mean?
options: In a right and equal way
questions.explanation: 'Fairly' means in a just and equal way. If you divide something 'fairly,' everyone gets the same — no one gets more or less than anyone else.

questions.question: When you are 'afraid,' you feel scared or frightened.
questions.explanation: Yes! 'Afraid' means feeling scared. The monkey said 'I am afraid that I ate too much' — but he was using it as a polite way to say sorry (while actually feeling quite happy!).

questions.question: I _______ my teacher for helping me with the difficult problem.
questions.explanation: 'Thanked' is the past tense of 'thank' — to express gratitude. The cats thanked the monkey each time he helped them divide the cheese.

questions.question: The monkey swung down from the tree. 'Swung' describes:
options: Moving back and forth (past tense of swing)
questions.explanation: 'Swung' is the past tense of 'swing' — to move by holding on and moving back and forth or from one place to another. The monkey swung from the tree branches to come down.

questions.question: The two pieces are _______ the same size — they are perfectly equal with no difference at all.
questions.explanation: 'Exactly' means perfectly, with absolutely no difference. 'Exactly the same' means the two things are a perfect match.

questions.question: The cats looked 'sadly' at their tiny pieces. Which word means the opposite of 'sadly'?
questions.explanation: 'Sadly' means in an unhappy way. The opposite is 'happily' — in a joyful way. The cats were sad, not happy, about their tiny pieces.

questions.question: 'Believe' means to think that something is true.
questions.explanation: Yes! 'Believe' means to accept something as true. The cats 'could not believe their good fortune' — they were so surprised it was hard to accept it was real.

questions.question: She carried the eggs _______ so they would not break.
questions.explanation: 'Carefully' means doing something with great attention to avoid mistakes or accidents. Handling fragile eggs carefully means you move slowly and gently.

questions.question: Sort these Week 4 words — are they ADVERBS (describe how) or VERBS (action words) or ADJECTIVES (describing words)?
questions.explanation: 'Fairly,' 'exactly,' and 'sadly' all describe how something is done (adverbs). 'Swung' and 'thanked' are actions (verbs). 'Afraid' describes a feeling (adjective).

questions.question: Which sentence uses 'carefully' correctly?
options: He walked carefully down the wet steps so he wouldn't slip.
We carefully afraid of the jungle.
questions.explanation: 'Carefully' describes how you do something. 'He walked carefully' — slowly and with attention — correctly uses it to modify the verb 'walked.'

questions.question: 'Fairly,' 'carefully,' 'exactly,' and 'sadly' are all adverbs — they end in '-ly.'
questions.explanation: Yes! Words ending in '-ly' are often adverbs. 'Fairly,' 'carefully,' 'exactly' (exception — no '-ly'), and 'sadly' describe how something is done. Note: 'exactly' ends in '-ly' too!

questions.question: Arrange the words to make a correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Correct sentence: 'The man walked carefully down the icy street.' 'Carefully' is the adverb that describes how he walked — with great care to avoid slipping.

questions.question: I don't _______ the monkey was honest — I think he was tricking the cats the whole time.
questions.explanation: 'Believe' means to accept something as true. 'I don't believe the monkey was honest' means you think he was lying — you do not accept his honesty as true.

questions.question: The monkey 'fairly' offered to help. In this sentence, what does 'fairly' tell us?
options: How the monkey made the offer — in a just, equal way
When the monkey made the offer
questions.explanation: 'Fairly' is an adverb — it tells us HOW the monkey made the offer. 'Fairly' = in a just and equal way. It answers the question 'How did the monkey offer?'

questions.question: Match each word to its OPPOSITE meaning.
questions.explanation: Opposites: afraid ↔ brave, fairly ↔ unfairly, sadly ↔ happily, carefully ↔ carelessly. Understanding opposites helps you remember what words mean!
